feature: Bulk Actions, Toasts Redesign, Tickets Details & Order Cancellation
scope: Bottom island (bulk actions) in Pedidos page
Order cancellation with mandatory reason and description

in_scope: Replacing 'Export CSV' with a new bulk action
Implementing a cancellable queue for bulk printing tickets
Redesigning `sonner` toasts to match premium aesthetics
Fixing thermal print CSS to avoid blank A4 rendering
Showing historical generated tickets in the order detail page
Updating DB schemas (Pedido) to store motivoCancelacion and descripcionCancelacion
Adding a UI modal/queue to input reason and description order-by-order when cancelling in bulk
Validating cancellation fields in frontend and backend
out_of_scope: Changes to other backend logic not related to tickets or cancellations

user_journeys.name: Cancelable Bulk Print
steps: User selects multiple orders in the table.
User clicks 'Imprimir Tickets' in the bottom island.
A queue starts processing orders sequentially.
System creates trackable tickets in the DB for each order.
User clicks 'Cancelar' on the progress indicator.
The queue stops immediately, preventing further tickets from generating.

user_journeys.name: View Generated Tickets
steps: User sees a new section listing all previously generated tickets for this order.

user_journeys.name: Order-by-Order Bulk Cancellation
steps: User selects multiple orders and clicks Cancel.
A wizard/carousel UI appears showing the first order to cancel.
User submits, the order is cancelled, and the wizard moves to the next order automatically.
Backend validates the inputs and updates the database.

acceptance_criteria: Given multiple selected orders, when clicking 'Imprimir Tickets', exactly 1 ticket is generated in the DB and printed for each selected order via a queue.
Given an active print queue, when the user clicks Cancel, the queue aborts.
Given the bottom island, the 'Exportar CSV' button is removed and replaced with a 'Cobrar Masivamente' action (which will open a modal to select the payment method).
Given the print dialog, the CSS strictly enforces thermal printer dimensions (e.g., 58mm/80mm) without rendering as an A4 page.
Given the order detail view, a section displays the list of generated tickets associated with the order.
Given an attempt to cancel multiple orders, the system requires a reason and description PER ORDER via a sequential UI, saving them to the DB.
Given any system action that triggers a Toast, the Toast appears with a premium glassmorphism design.
ux_behavior: Queue Progress: A fixed or floating UI element showing progress.
Toasts: Smooth GSAP-like entry, blur background, distinct success/error colors, premium typography.
Cancellation Carousel: A sequential wizard showing the current order being cancelled, allowing distinct inputs for each.
